title: Presented by Bottega
description: Another fancy slideshow by Bottega, proving once more that they were so right to leave Instagram!!!!!!

worldSystems.name: Fan Shrine
worldSystems.reason: tags include fashion; archetype affinity: commerce shrine stage

worldSystems.name: Luxury Archive
worldSystems.reason: tags include fashion; archetype affinity: commerce shrine stage

mechanics.interactionModel: Objects are treated like collectibles or relics; product rails, badges, and states carry as much narrative weight as the checkout.
mechanics.navigationModel: Browse objects first, then enter detail or purchase state without losing the collection context.
mechanics.stateModel: Collection browse -> featured object focus -> detail/purchase -> return to collection.
engineHints: Use server-rendered product data or commerce primitives under heavily authored cards and rails.
Keep product metadata, release status, and edition cues visible in the browsing view.
Use hover or selection states to deepen object presence before checkout.
motionRecipe: Use hover lift, collectible flips, and ambient drift around featured objects.
Keep cart or checkout transitions legible and calm.
Treat release counters and stock states like part of the scene.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Use a single-column collectible feed with sticky purchase rails and clear release-state chips instead of shrinking desktop grids.
implementationNotes: Preserve collection logic in the browse state so the page feels like a shrine, not a commodity grid.
Use release, edition, or provenance metadata as part of the emotional pitch.
Make cart and checkout states calmer than the browsing world.
antiPatterns: Do not flatten the page into a normal storefront once products appear.
Do not hide pricing or size details until too late.
Do not let the shrine fiction block purchase clarity.
validationChecklist: Product browse feels collectible before it feels transactional.
Release or edition state is visible in the browse view.
Mobile keeps the collection context while making checkout simpler.

designGuidance.implementationPrompt: Build this as a Fan Shrine page with a secondary cue from Luxury Archive using the Commerce Shrine Stage interaction model. Keep the extracted primary font as the voice anchor, preserve the palette around #fefefe, #dcdede, #acaeaf, drive the page through scroll, tap, hover, and implement the mobile fallback as: Use a single-column collectible feed with sticky purchase rails and clear release-state chips instead of shrinking desktop grids.
designGuidance.summary: Fan Shrine + Commerce Shrine Stage. Objects are treated like collectibles or relics; product rails, badges, and states carry as much narrative weight as the checkout.
